title: Upon Roots. Epig.
content:
Roots had no money; yet he went o' the score,
    For a wrought purse; can any tell wherefore?
    Say, what should Roots do with a purse in print,
    That had not gold nor silver to put in't?
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon Roots. Epig.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
